label: 12-month ADL/IADL loss rate
source_oecd: OECD Health Statistics Table 10.2 (long-term care recipients)
source_nonooecd: WHO-SAGE wave data + admin imputation (see note 1)

label: Preventable hospitalisation rate
source_oecd: OECD Health at a Glance Table 6.3 (ACSC admissions)
source_nonooecd: WHO Global Health Observatory ACS indicator
notes: Ambulatory care sensitive conditions per OECD 2017 definition

label: Care-dependency escalation rate
source_oecd: OECD LTC Statistics DB Table LTC_RECIP
source_nonooecd: World Bank HNP – Disability & LTC indicators (series HD.HCI)
notes: Transition from lower to higher dependency classification

label: Social care withdrawal rate
source_oecd: OECD LTC Statistics DB Table LTC_RCPT_CARE_TYPE
source_nonooecd: ILO World Social Protection DB – active beneficiary series
notes: Unplanned cessation of assessed care plan; not available pre-2010 for CHARLS/LASI countries — see note 2

label: Institutional Decoupling Score (primary)
source_oecd: ESI: OECD Rec Rights DB; GIS: Delphi expert panel (n=36)
source_nonooecd: ESI: ILO WSPR chapters + WHO LTC country profiles; GIS: same Delphi panel

label: Institutional Decoupling Score (robustness)
source_oecd: Same as IDS_pure + alpha calibrated by CV-MSE 2000-2009
notes: Alpha endogenous to FEI; not used for primary causal inference

label: Care-Layer Slippage
source_oecd: Boundary fidelity weights from OECD PaperMon + MISSOC
source_nonooecd: WHO LTC governance scores + ILO WSPR governance indicator
notes: CLS = mean(1-w_ij) + 0.43*H_B(G); lambda calibrated by penalised MLE

label: Log GDP per capita (2015 PPP USD)
LTC expenditure as share of GDP
source_nonooecd: WHO-GHED LTC functional category (where available); World Bank HNP elsewhere

label: Government effectiveness
source_oecd: World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators GE.EST

label: FEI construction for pre-cohort years
notes: For China 2000-2010 (pre-CHARLS) and India 2000-2016 (pre-LASI): FEI items 1-3 are constructed from WHO-SAGE cross-sectional age-standardised estimates and national Ministry of Health administrative records; item 4 (social care withdrawal) is treated as missing and imputed using within-country linear interpolation anchored at the first cohort wave. Sensitivity analysis excluding pre-cohort years yielded beta=0.144 (WCB CI: 0.098-0.191).

label: Social care withdrawal coverage
notes: Item 4 is available from 2009 onwards for OECD countries (OECD LTC Statistics DB first systematic collection). For non-OECD countries item 4 is available from the first cohort wave onwards. The 3-item FEI (omitting item 4) is the pre-registered primary specification; item 4 is included in the 4-indicator measurement sensitivity specification.
